author: @OMGWTFBBQKITTEN
text:
WWE DEI Champion? 

How many times was Hulk Hogan put into a sleeper hold to eventually snap out of it for an over the top adrenaline rush comeback to win? So that dude cant hang for 2 seconds and just taps out? Sounds pretty lame for a Wrestlemania match win. Traditionally in WWE finishing moves win the match in the weekly matches, but in Wrestlemania traditional finishing moves including the once devastating DDT are not always a guaranteed win, so how is a sleeper hold tap out in Wrestlemania even possible. It sounds so bad, like a cash grab match that they didnt have time to work on a worthy match climax.

author: @OwocowyMr
text: I think night one was okay at best. In regards to Jey Uso, I'm happy for him, he worked his ass off throughout last year, love him or hate him. His win was obvious but I don't like that they made Gunther tap out almosr instantly, they should've make him pass out after half a minute like they did with Cody and others to make them look strong despite loosing. But then again, there's no way in hell he was "burried", he's gonna get back on top one day, he is still one of HHH's most trusted workers so he's gonna get the belt back one way or another, or perhaps they'll push him to the WWE championship picture. After all, the draft is coming soon so the rosters might shake up a bit. And as for Jey, I dont think his reign is gonna last longer than SummerSlam, which is a proper length for a first ever world title reign IMO, not everybody has to wear a belt for a year or so
